title: stanleya pinnata
intro: Stanleya pinnata, also known as Horse Gentian, is a perennial plant native to the Great Basin region of North America, characterized by its tall stem and yellow or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and indigestion, due to its bitter compounds. The health benefits of Stanleya pinnata include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated tissues and skin. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
